title: Uncertainty-Aware Risk-Value ALNS for Vehicle-UAV Inspection
subtitle: A research-code project page for the manuscript-facing mathematical model, proposed algorithm, and experiment evidence.
venue: Manuscript-facing code and reproducibility evidence, rebuilt 2026-06-25

teaser.alt: Algorithm comparison figure generated from project experiment results.
teaser.caption: Main experiment evidence: Proposed ALNS is compared against external classical and strong metaheuristic baselines.

abstract: This project implements the final manuscript-facing vehicle-UAV inspection model with same-stop multi-tower sortie patterns, q95 energy screening, risk-value scheduling, schedule-state adaptive large-neighborhood search, and a metric-aware final portfolio. The rebuilt main experiment compares the complete proposed method against external baselines only; internal ALNS variants are reserved for ablation.

result_highlights.label: P2 main experiment
result_highlights.value: 640 rows from 10 matched seeds across eight corridor scales.

result_highlights.label: RWCT gain
result_highlights.value: 17.44% to 40.70% mean RWCT reduction versus the strongest external baseline at each scale.

result_highlights.label: Matched wins
result_highlights.value: Proposed beats every external baseline in all 80 matched seed-scale cases.

result_highlights.label: Statistical evidence
result_highlights.value: Holm-adjusted paired RWCT p value is 0.01367 against the strongest external baseline at each scale.

result_highlights.label: Feasibility
result_highlights.value: Proposed reports zero infeasible-sortie rate across all P2 scales in the full run.

sections.title: Mathematical Model
sections.summary: The code uses explicit same-stop sortie patterns and a finite service set. Each selected pattern is evaluated with duration, mean energy, q95 energy, completion time, and risk-value contribution.
bullets: Decision layer: assign inspection towers to vehicle stops, UAVs, and bounded same-stop sortie patterns.
Objective evidence: makespan, RWCT, vehicle distance, q95 energy, missed service, duplicate service, and feasibility penalties are exported as diagnostics.
Uncertainty layer: q95 energy screening applies the battery reserve before schedule-state ALNS repair accepts a pattern.

sections.title: Proposed Algorithm
sections.summary: The proposed method is the complete q95 risk-value schedule-state ALNS with adaptive destroy-repair search and final portfolio selection.
bullets: Destroy operators include random, worst-energy, related, path-segment, and UAV-chain removal.
Repair operators include greedy, regret-k, energy-minimum, and synchronization-aware insertion.
Final selection is lexicographic: infeasible-sortie count, then RWCT, then makespan.
Risk-bucketed stop grouping is used as a portfolio candidate at large scales.

sections.title: Main Experiment
sections.summary: P2 is now a true main experiment: external baselines versus the complete proposed method.
bullets: External baselines: Nearest, GA, ACO, simulated annealing, tabu search, VNS, and HGS-VNS.
Internal ALNS variants are excluded from P2 and moved to P4.
The regenerated manuscript table is available at results/tables/table_algorithm_results.tex.

sections.title: Ablation Study
sections.summary: Ablation is interpreted only after the main experiment establishes the proposed method against external baselines.
bullets: Internal variants: fixed ALNS, point-energy ALNS, and UQ-energy ALNS.
Module removals: no surrogate, no UQ, no risk-value, no adaptive scoring, no clustering, no energy repair, and no sync repair.
Ablation evidence is reported separately from the main algorithm-comparison claim.

sections.title: Public GIS Scenarios
sections.summary: Public GIS cases use public geometry and weather inputs while keeping risk, value, and some stop attributes as proxy-generated labels.
bullets: The public-case runner now uses the same external-baseline method set as P2.
GIS evidence is useful for geometry-grounded scheduling checks, not utility field validation.
The AirLab branch supports energy calibration only, not full transmission-line field deployment claims.

sections.title: Evidence Boundary
sections.summary: The retained claim is RWCT dominance against external baselines in the rebuilt P2 run. Other metrics must be stated with their actual support.
bullets: Top-risk coverage is not uniformly best at small and medium scales.
The current data support simulation and GIS-grounded proxy evidence, not real utility field validation.
Legacy P2 files that compare ALNS-family variants should not be used as final main-experiment evidence.

sections.title: Code and Documentation
sections.summary: The project is organized as one shared research-code package with tests, result tables, source data, and manuscript-facing documentation.
bullets: Model notes: docs/model.md
Algorithm notes: docs/algorithm.md
API notes: docs/api.md
Experiment notes: docs/experiments.md
Claim ledger: docs/claim_algorithm_evidence_ledger.md

boundaries: Top-risk coverage is not uniformly best at small and medium scales.
Public GIS cases use public geometry and weather, but inspection risk and task value remain proxy labels.
AirLab telemetry validates the energy branch only; it is not full utility inspection field validation.
